desc: collectValues reuses one mutable default list across calls, causing values and external mutations to leak between invocations.
The bare except catches BaseException subclasses such as KeyboardInterrupt and SystemExit and silently converts them into a zero value.
doStuff mutates global total_result, making results depend on prior calls and causing lost updates under concurrent execution.
processEverything silently truncates every float to an integer, so fractional input data is discarded.
Exact type comparisons reject valid int, float, and str subclasses and unexpectedly treat them as zero.
Converting NaN or infinite float inputs with int(item) raises an uncaught ValueError or OverflowError.
Hardcoded approximations of pi and e introduce avoidable numerical error despite importing the more accurate math constants.
doStuff unconditionally sleeps for 10 milliseconds per item, imposing linear latency unrelated to its computation.
doStuff takes ten weakly related parameters and uses deeply nested boolean branching, making behavior difficult to understand, test, and safely modify.
Parameters i and j have no observable effect because their only branch executes pass.
The temp1 and temp2 operations cancel each other exactly and add unnecessary computation and cognitive overhead.
Converting the numeric total to a string and immediately back to float is redundant and can unnecessarily discard integer precision.
Assigning to sum shadows Python's built-in sum function and makes later maintenance more error-prone.
